response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced full-stack and web developer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role across multiple hard requirements. Most critically, he lists no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a non-negotiable requirement for this position. His C experience is limited to a brief stint writing IDE components at National Instruments and a personal C++ audio project, with no evidence of Linux kernel-level work, embedded networking, or switch firmware development. He has no documented experience with any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 networking, and his embedded systems exposure is peripheral at best.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts demonstrates general technical adaptability
Early C/C++ work at National Instruments and a Linux-based wave field synthesis thesis project show at least baseline familiarity with lower-level systems programming
Strong DevOps and infrastructure background (CI/CD, Docker, GitLab, Kubernetes) could add operational value in a team environment
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a stated native-level requirement — makes this candidate ineligible without exception
No Linux kernel modification, embedded firmware, or network switch experience; the resume is overwhelmingly web and application-layer focused
No exposure to any required security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, MAC authentication, encryption key exchange, device attack detection)

key_factors.factor: No embedded security or networking domain knowledge
